summary: A custom embedded-locus kind gains an optional render(value) hook that replaces the default TOML fence view for its blocks() values; absent, projection is byte-unchanged (0018 slice 2/3)

edit.description: `KindDefinition<T>` gains an optional `render?: (value: EmbeddedMemberValue) => string` — the kind author's own composed view of an embedded member's value, carried alongside `facts`/`key` the way `kind()` (line ~155) already returns them; erased at the seam, so the engine only ever sees the resulting string. While this file is open: `EmbeddedMemberValue`'s own doc comment (~187) still says the rendered fence is 'read back byte-identically by the engine's `parse_embedded_member` fold' — that fold (plus `parse_embedded_info`) was retired with no fallback by RETIRE-FOLD-MEMBERS (5ee0b6d); the comment is now factually wrong against pipeline.md 'Emit' (an embedded member's facts are lock declaration rows, never mined back from its rendering). Correct it in passing (residue found at plan 52b3dcd).

`resolveBody`'s `blocks` branch / `renderMemberFence` (~93-122, today always `renderMemberToml`'s default `[collection.entry]` view) checks whether the value's originating kind declared a `render` hook and calls it instead when present; the outer `member.<kind> <key>` fence wrapper and mention/leaf resolution discipline are unchanged. Same residue as kind.ts: `renderMemberToml`'s and `renderMemberFence`'s own doc comments (~68, ~90) cite the retired `parse_embedded_member`/`parse_embedded_info` fold as what reads the fence back into leaves/members — stale for the same reason; fix both while touching these two functions (residue found at plan 52b3dcd).

tests.asserts: grows a case asserting a `kind()` with a `render` hook emits its custom string inside the `member.<kind> <key>` fence in place of the default TOML view, and a `kind()` without one keeps today's byte-identical default — regression on 'a blocks() body renders an embedded member as a member.<kind> <key> TOML fence' (~462).

acceptance: A project-declared embedded kind supplying render() sees its output verbatim inside the member fence at emit; a kind supplying none is byte-identical to today's projection — zero forced migration.
notes: Slice 2 of 3 for 0018's carried inbox scope (filed 6bed388; cite Consequences: an embedded-locus kind's format is 'writer-only and unconstrained... no admissibility bar'). Slice 1 (NESTED-MEMBER-COLLECTIONS-ORDERED) shipped 80697f8; ship-audit-verified at 80697f8 (cargo test, sdk `node --test`, and `tsc --noEmit` all green; collections confirmed an ordered {key,leaves} list, cited line refs above still resolve) — gate flips open. Scoped at 6bed388.

summary: An embedded member's leaf may be a Text (template + mentions), not just a bare string; its mentions lift into the host's mention rows, its resolved display becomes the stored leaf (0018 slice 3/3)

edit.description: `EmbeddedMemberValue.leaves`, and a collection entry's leaves, widen from `Readonly<Record<string,string>>` to accept a `Text` (imported from `prose.ts`) per leaf alongside a bare string; `embeddedMemberValue()`'s init type follows.

`renderMemberToml`'s leaf loop (top-level and the nested collection-entry loop, ~72-81) resolves a `Text` leaf the way `resolveBody` resolves member-level `Text` — mention resolution-checked against `options.mentionable` (loud on a dangling address, contract.md 'edge'), rendered by the one display rule — before TOML-string-quoting it; a bare-string leaf is unchanged.

`mentionRows()` (~354-383, today walks only top-level member prose) also walks each `blocks()`-declared `EmbeddedMemberValue`'s `Text` leaves, emitting a `MentionRow` per mention keyed to the leaf's own address — the existing `<member>/<kind>/<key>/<child-path>` leaf-address grammar `src/read.rs`'s `parse_leaf_address` already resolves, not a new one.

tests.asserts: grows a case asserting a Text-valued leaf's mention resolves and renders inline, and an unresolved mention inside a leaf is a loud emit error, mirroring 'a resolved mention renders to its declared value's display form' (~442) / 'an unresolved mention is a loud emit error' (~454) at leaf grain.

'leaf_addresses_are_structural_member_kind_key_child_path' (~118, shifted by NESTED-MEMBER-COLLECTIONS-ORDERED's ordered-collections rework) grows a case where the addressed leaf itself carries a resolved mention, asserting explain/impact still read its plain resolved string.

acceptance: An embedded member's leaf authored as a text-tagged template with a mention emits with the mention resolved and displayed, contributes a mention row addressed to that leaf, and an unresolved leaf mention refuses emit exactly like a member-level one; a bare-string leaf is unchanged.
notes: Slice 3 of 3 for 0018's carried inbox scope (filed 6bed388, cite Consequences). Depends on nothing from slices 1/2 semantically but shares sdk/src/kind.ts + emit.ts + declarations.ts, so blockedBy slice 2 per entry-discipline file-disjointness, matching the inbox's stated do-last order. Scoped at 6bed388.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at 43b3471 (quiet closing pass): still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
